problem.title: Best Meeting Point
problem.description: Given a binary grid where 1 represents a person and 0 represents an empty space, find a meeting point that minimizes the total Manhattan distance from all people to the meeting point. Return the minimum total distance.
assumptions: At least one person exists in the grid.
The optimal meeting point does not need to be on a cell with a person.

example.explanation: The optimal meeting point is (0,2) or (1,2). Total distance = 2+2+2 = 6.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Median Minimizes Sum of Absolute Deviations
solutions.description: Collect the row indices and column indices of all people separately. The point minimizing total Manhattan distance has its row coordinate equal to the median of all row indices and column coordinate equal to the median of all column indices — this is because Manhattan distance decomposes into independent row and column components. Collect row indices in sorted order by scanning top-to-bottom; sort column indices. The total distance is the sum of absolute differences from each person's row/col to the median row/col.

complexity.time: O(mn log mn) dominated by sorting column indices; row indices come pre-sorted
complexity.space: O(k) where k is the number of people

example_walkthrough.text: People at (0,0), (0,4), (2,2). rows=[0,0,2], cols=[0,2,4] (after sort). Median row = 0. Median col = 2. Row dist: (0-0)+(2-0)=2. Col dist: (2-0)+(4-2)=4. Total = 6.
